question: Which of the six archived MEV opportunity classes are recurring, public enough to observe from Xatu data, and not obviously dead on arrival given the competitive landscape?
hypothesis: Of the six archived classes, only a subset will pass a multi-dimensional feasibility screen. Structural/systemic classes relying on fully public relay data will score highest; classes requiring private order flow access or dominated by Tier 1 builders will be assessed as infeasible for independent strategy development.

decision.rationale: The feasibility layer separates the six archived opportunity classes into three tiers: two fully feasible for research/analytics (builder market position, validator dependency risk), two marginal (ETH arbitrage detection, liquidation monitoring), and two dead on arrival for independent execution (sandwich, backrunning). The observability and competition filters produce a clear, evidence-backed ranking that constrains the strategy space for downstream work.

reproducibility.data_source: Synthesis of 7 prior validated runs from Xatu public parquet export
reproducibility.notes: No new parquet queries executed. Feasibility assessment applies structured scoring to the Opportunity Archive (Run 7) using evidence from all prior runs.
